<!-- generated from maestro item 315 — do not edit; the board item is the source of truth -->
# OAuth callback fires completeOAuthLogin twice — login.html duplicates auth_base's generic handler, double-spending the single-use code

- **Kind**: bug
- **Date**: 2026-07-24
- **Requested by**: Ian Starnes (spotted by Claude while scoping board item 75, filed on Ian's request)

## Description

When an OAuth provider redirects back to the hosted login page with `?code=&state=`, TWO handlers
each detect the params and each call `MojoAuth.completeOAuthLogin(provider)`:

1. `mojo/apps/account/templates/account/auth_base.html:207-219` — the generic handler (runs on
   every page extending the base): reads `oauth_provider` from sessionStorage, **removes it**,
   spins the `btn-<provider>` button, calls complete, and on failure clears the spinner and shows
   a provider-specific error.
2. `mojo/apps/account/templates/account/login.html:241-249` (page_script block) — a redundant
   subset: re-reads `oauth_provider` (already removed by handler 1 → falls back to `"google"`),
   calls complete again, no spinner handling.

Neither handler strips `code`/`state` from the shared `URLSearchParams` (`_matConfig.params`) or
guards against the other, so both always fire. The same single-use authorization code is POSTed
twice — the second time to the WRONG provider endpoint (`google` fallback) for Apple/GitHub logins.

## Context

Observed effects on every hosted-page OAuth sign-in:

- A guaranteed spurious failed completion server-side (used code / wrong provider) — noise in
  auth-failure accounting (cf. `tests/test_incident/test_auth_failures_metric.py`) and logs.
- The losing call renders an error message behind the success overlay (mostly invisible, but
  present).
- A real, if rare, race: auth_base's fetch is issued first but ordering isn't guaranteed. If the
  login.html call (provider defaulted to `"google"`) reaches the server first for an Apple/GitHub
  login and consumes the single-use state/code record, the CORRECT completion then fails — a
  hard sign-in failure for that round-trip.

History: both blocks shipped together in the original hosted-templates commit (`fa05f339`,
2026-03-22, ~v1.0.79) — original duplication, not recent churn from ITEM-026/ITEM-034.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Exactly one `completeOAuthLogin` call site remains across the hosted-page templates (the
      generic `auth_base.html` handler; `register.html`/`contact.html` have none — verified).
- [ ] A `?code&state` callback issues exactly one `POST /api/auth/oauth/<provider>/complete`, to
      the provider stored at begin time — no `"google"` fallback misfire for Apple/GitHub.
- [ ] Login-page UX preserved: provider button spinner + "Completing sign-in…" info message +
      error rendering (all already provided by the auth_base handler).
- [ ] Regression test fails if a second completion call site reappears in a rendered page
      (render-assertion pattern from `tests/test_auth/login_methods.py` `_render_login()`; e.g.
      rendered HTML contains exactly one `completeOAuthLogin(`).

## Investigation

- **What exists**: two independent `?code&state` handlers, `auth_base.html:207-219` (generic,
  full-featured) and `login.html:241-249` (subset, wrong-provider fallback after sessionStorage
  consumption). Only these two call sites exist in templates (grep-verified);
  `mojo-auth.js:689` is the library method itself.
- **What changes**: remove the `login.html:240-249` duplicate block (auth_base's handler is a
  strict superset: spinner, provider cleanup, provider-specific fallback copy). Add the
  regression test.
- **Constraints**: auth_base's inline script executes before the page_script block, so today the
  correct-provider call is issued first and usually wins — which is why OAuth "works" in the
  field. Server-side single-use state semantics are what make the loser fail; do NOT change
  server-side state handling for this item.
- **Related files**: `mojo/apps/account/templates/account/login.html`,
  `mojo/apps/account/templates/account/auth_base.html`, `tests/test_auth/login_methods.py`
  (test pattern), `docs/web_developer/account/auth_pages.md` (OAuth flow description, if the
  described flow mentions the page JS).
- **Out of scope**: board item 75 (passkey/WebAuthn error UX — touches the same two files; no
  dependency either way, whichever builds second rebases trivially); any server-side OAuth
  state/replay changes; the deprecated `completeGoogleLogin` alias in mojo-auth.js.

## Plan

_Scoped 2026-07-24 in django-mojo at HEAD (904d8e12). Re-verified this session: the only two
template call sites are `auth_base.html:213` and `login.html:246`; `oauthCode`/`oauthState`
(:241-242) and the block-local `provider` (:244) are referenced nowhere else in login.html's
page script; `register.html`/`contact.html`/`passkey_enroll.html` have no `?code&state` handling
of their own._

### Objective

A `?code&state` OAuth callback on any hosted auth page triggers exactly one
`completeOAuthLogin(provider)` call — the generic `auth_base.html` handler, with the provider
recorded at begin time. The duplicate (and wrong-provider-after-sessionStorage-consumption) POST
from login.html is gone. No user-visible UX change: spinner, "Completing sign-in…" info message,
success overlay, and error rendering all already live in the surviving handler.

### Design decision — delete login.html's block, keep auth_base's

The auth_base handler is a strict superset (provider-button spinner on/off, sessionStorage
cleanup, provider-specific error fallback `provider + " sign-in failed."`) and it runs on every
page extending the base — register.html gets OAuth-callback handling from it too, so completion
logic must stay there. login.html's block adds nothing and reads `oauth_provider` AFTER
auth_base removed it (guaranteed `"google"` fallback). Alternatives rejected:
- De-duping via a guard flag (e.g. `window._oauthHandled`) — keeps dead code alive to protect
  against itself; pointless once the duplicate is gone.
- Moving handling INTO login.html and out of auth_base — would silently drop OAuth-callback
  handling on register.html (its OAuth buttons round-trip back to the register page).

### Implementation steps

1. **`mojo/apps/account/templates/account/login.html`** — delete lines 240-249 wholesale: the
   `// OAuth callback — provider redirects back with ?code=&state=` comment, the
   `oauthCode`/`oauthState` declarations, and the `if (oauthCode && oauthState) {...}` block
   (its `m.showMessage("Completing sign-in…")` + `completeOAuthLogin` + catch). Nothing else in
   the page script references any of these names (verified). Do NOT touch the `pr:` token block
   (:251-258) or anything else.
2. **`mojo/apps/account/templates/account/auth_base.html`** — no changes. The handler at
   :207-219 is the single surviving completion path.
3. **CHANGELOG.md** — `**fix**` entry in the rolling top block: hosted login page no longer
   double-fires OAuth completion (single-use code was POSTed twice, second time to the `google`
   endpoint for Apple/GitHub logins; rare race could fail the sign-in, and every OAuth login
   produced a spurious failed-completion server-side).

### Edge cases (all unchanged behavior, verified against the surviving handler)

- **Stale/bookmarked callback URL** (refresh with used `?code&state`): fires once, fails once,
  shows one error — previously showed the same but doubled. Acceptable, unchanged semantics.
- **Session-check skip** (`auth_base.html:174` skips auto-refresh when `code`+`state` present):
  reads the params directly, independent of the deleted block — unaffected.
- **`oauth_provider` missing from sessionStorage** (cross-tab/cleared): auth_base's own
  `|| "google"` fallback remains — pre-existing behavior, explicitly out of scope.
- **register.html OAuth round-trip**: handled by auth_base before and after — unaffected.

### Testing

Baseline first per `.claude/rules/build-baseline.md`: `bin/run_tests --agent`, read
`var/test_failures.json`, record in the item's activity trail.

New file `tests/test_auth/oauth_callback_single_fire.py` (read
`docs/django_developer/testit/Overview.md` first; every assert gets a failure message; no DB
fixtures needed — render with `group=None`, so no setup/cleanup). Borrow the generic
`_render(template_name, group=None)` helper from `tests/test_register/passkey_prompt.py:44-53`
(RequestFactory + `_auth_context` + `render()`), not the login-only variant. Three tests:

1. **Rendered login page has exactly one completion call** — `_render('account/login.html')`
   HTML contains exactly 1 occurrence of `completeOAuthLogin(`. Fails on old code (2), passes
   on fix (1 — auth_base's). This is the core regression.
2. **Rendered register page has exactly one completion call** — `_render('account/register.html')`
   count == 1: proves auth_base still ships the handler everywhere (guards against "fixing" by
   deleting the wrong block).
3. **login.html template source has zero call sites** — package-relative read of
   `mojo/apps/account/templates/account/login.html` (path via
   `os.path.dirname(mojo.apps.account.__file__)`, same pattern as
   `tests/test_auth/login_methods.py:215-229`) asserts `completeOAuthLogin` absent — pins WHERE
   the single call lives so the dup can't return under a different render path.

Run `bin/run_tests --agent -t test_auth.oauth_callback_single_fire`, then the default suite —
green-to-green vs the baseline. No schema changes → no `bin/create_testproject`. True
end-to-end double-POST observation needs a browser; the render/source assertions plus the
untouched server-side OAuth tests (`tests/test_oauth/`) cover the repo-side contract.

### Documentation

- No doc-track changes: `docs/web_developer/account/auth_pages.md` "OAuth Flow" (:161-168)
  describes the intended single-completion flow generically ("The page JS detects the
  `code` + `state` params and calls `MojoAuth.completeOAuthLogin()`") — accurate after the fix;
  it never documented the duplicate. `docs/django_developer/account/auth_pages.md` likewise
  describes flow, not the per-template JS layout.
- CHANGELOG entry per step 3.

### Interaction with item 75

Item 75 (passkey error UX, `planned`) edits the same two files in different regions (login.html
passkey catch ~:298, auth_base showMessage :107). No dependency either way; deleting :240-249
shifts later line numbers by ~10, so whichever builds second re-anchors by content, not line
number. Both plans note this.
